domain: Etruscan haruspex Liver of Piacenza (bronze 16-section sheep liver; each section = named deity; trained priest reads sacrificed liver via fixed-template anatomical-zone lookup; output is structured 16-tuple deity-judgment)
specific_mechanism: Fixed K-zone deterministic template with per-zone named criterion; output is K-tuple of independent zone judgments; no free-form holistic interpretation allowed.
llm_application: HARUSPEX-EVAL: a deterministic K-zone evaluation rubric where each zone is a named criterion with a fixed scoring scale (e.g., 16 dimensions: factuality, hallucination-rate, coverage, citation-quality, structure, code-correctness, tool-use-fidelity, instruction-following, etc.); the judge LLM is COMPELLED via constrained decoding to emit a strict K-tuple judgment (one score per zone with no holistic aggregation in the same pass); a separate (different model) downstream aggregator combines the K-tuple into a single decision using a fixed lookup table (not learned). The KEY is the SEPARATION of per-zone judgment (constrained-deterministic) from aggregation (lookup-table-deterministic), and the K-zone template is FROZEN across evaluations.
novelty_claim: G-Eval gives free-form CoT-based scoring (not deterministic). DAGMetric uses DAG-based step-decisions (closer but free-form within steps). Rubric-Is-All-You-Need uses question-specific rubrics (not frozen template). Analytic Rubric scores criterion-by-criterion but uses free-form judge. HARUSPEX-EVAL differs by FROZEN K-ZONE TEMPLATE + CONSTRAINED-DECODING-FORCED K-TUPLE + LOOKUP-TABLE AGGREGATION (no aggregator LLM), giving fully deterministic non-learned eval.
